coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Vert work day. Find a steep grade and do 4–6 x 5 min uphill efforts at strong effort (Z3–Z4), walking back down as full recovery. Target 800–1,200 ft of gain total. Keep the top of each rep honest but not maxed — the goal is leg-strength stimulus and hip-flexor drive, not speed. This is key prep for Kranjska Gora's opening climb. If you're on a flat area, substitute 30 min of stair repeats at a tall building. Finish with 10 min easy Z1–Z2 cooldown on flat ground.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Vert days burn more than they feel like — uphill running is roughly 1.5x the caloric cost of flat running per mile. Hit 2,400–2,700 cal today. Load carbs in the 2 hours before (oatmeal, banana, toast) and refuel within 30 min post-run with 30–40 g protein plus 60–80 g fast carbs. Hydration matters more on sustained climbs — drink 500 ml 90 min before and carry water for anything over 60 min.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Downhill walking recovery between reps is active rest — keep moving, don't stand still. Post-run, elevate legs 10–15 min and spend 5 min on hip flexor and glute stretches (lunges, pigeon). Quads will be loaded tonight; a contrast shower (90 sec cold after warm) accelerates flush. Sleep 8+ hours — vert work creates more muscle damage than flat running and the rebuild happens overnight.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: The Julian Alps 50K has 8,000+ ft of gain. Every vert session is a direct deposit into race-day confidence. Today's reps simulate the lung burn and leg fatigue of the first big climb — learn to stay smooth and controlled when it hurts uphill. Breathe through the nose on the lower efforts; switch to mouth when it spikes. That control is what separates finishers from DNFs on alpine courses.
